description: 5-class hallucination taxonomy + Therasense kill-switch on fabricated prior-art citations. Class A (fabricated prior-art) is an INSTANT FAIL on the entire draft — the kill-switch is non-negotiable per Therasense v. Becton Dickinson (Fed. Cir. 2011) inequitable-conduct doctrine.
sub_criteria: Class A — Fabricated prior-art citation (patent number / publication that does not exist)
Class B — Misattributed prior art (citation exists but does not teach what is claimed)
Class C — Ungrounded technical claim (spec asserts a property not supported by disclosure)
Class D — Inconsistent reference (internal contradiction in disclosure)
Class E — Overreach (claim scope outside what disclosure supports — masked §112(a) defect)

scoring_levels.label: Excellent
scoring_levels.criteria: Zero hallucinations across Classes A-E. All cited prior-art numbers verify against USPTO Patent Public Search.

scoring_levels.label: Good
scoring_levels.criteria: Zero Class A. ≤1 Class B-E finding (e.g., one minor misattribution).

scoring_levels.label: Adequate
scoring_levels.criteria: Zero Class A. 2-3 Class B-E findings, none affecting claim validity.

scoring_levels.label: Weak
scoring_levels.criteria: Zero Class A. Multiple Class B-E findings, including at least one Class C ungrounded claim.

scoring_levels.label: Unacceptable
scoring_levels.criteria: Class A triggered → Therasense kill-switch → instant fail on entire draft, regardless of Layer 1-4 scores. This is a hard floor.

track_a_signals: USPTO Patent Public Search verification per cited patent number
track_b_protocol: Reviewer records Class B-E findings during ship-readiness review. Class A is detected automatically by the harness Layer 5 — reviewer confirms but is not the primary detector.

kill_switch.name: Therasense
kill_switch.trigger: Any Class A fabricated prior-art citation
kill_switch.effect: Instant fail on entire draft; composite score floor at 1.0; finding logged with reference, location, and verification record
